summary_text: **Revised plan (simulation lane, pre-implementation only)** 1) **Initialize with fixed mode and source boundary** - Set mode to **draft-only** (no scheduling, no publish) unless user later requests otherwise. - Classify this as **note lead-post flow** (Forbes→note URL provided). Use the source URL in `Body`/`Body JA` and `Source URLs`; do not treat it as quote-reply mode. 2) **Source-grounded extraction pass (hard fail on missing evidence)** - Pull canonical content from the URL and capture: headline intent, 3–5 core claims, and one direct evidence sentence per claim. - Preserve exact URLs and timestamps; strip tracking params if needed for consistency in `Source URLs`. - If extraction is blocked (paywall/access error, short page), pause and request user direction before drafting. 3) **Draft construction with abstraction constraints** - Produce two deliverables: `Body` (JP) and `Body JA` (if bilingual workflow requires it). - Draft length target: **~800–1000 Japanese characters** for schedulable-ready candidate. - Do not include branded proper nouns, names, handles, slogans, or explicit promotional framing unless explicitly approved. - If a claim requires specificity, push specificity into `Source URLs` and/or internal notes, not publish-facing text. 4) **Preflight validation sequence before any handoff** - Posting invariants: mode=draft, queue target clear, no pending schedule. - Body-mode checks: no `x.com` / `twitter.com` links in main body for this flow. - Proper-noun leakage scan on `Body`, `Body JA`, and any thumbnail-related text. - `Source URLs` populated with canonical article link; if lead-post, URL also appears in body. - Runtime check only if needed: current x-auto runtime state and Notion row readiness before claiming completion. 5) **Deliverable shape** - Final artifact should be: - `Body` draft (single coherent post, plain text only) - optional `Body JA` equivalent - `Source URLs` field only - short “validation note” with extracted claim list and leak-check pass/fail. 6) **Weak spots to preempt** - Sequence risk: extracting and drafting before leak checks can lock in forbidden branded language. - Validation risk: treating it as quote-post and leaving x.com links in body. - Content risk: under-length draft that passes review textually but fails scheduler/runtime expectations. - Source risk: stale/incorrect URL in `Source URLs`, or claim text copied without source evidence.
